reviews.review_msg: He made a course very content-heavy and unclear. The grading criteria is unclear it felt like he always tries to catch students' mistakes so because of that he doesn't provide a clear rubric. Lectures are so confusing and he expects full participation. I learned everything the night before the exam and realized the content is not that hard.

Paul is a kind professor but his marking is extremely vague and hard to get anything more than an A-. For the group project, he made us switch a portion of it days before it was due because he was planning on teaching that concept and yet, he didn't end up teaching it. I would avoid taking this class with Paul especially if it's not a requirement.

Save yourself the headache - Marks extremely hard and feels unfair for someone who doesn't provide rubrics for half the assignments. No one in the class got 10/10 for participation despite attending all classes and actively participating. Paul seems like a genuinely kind and caring individual, but the poor teaching cannot be overlooked. Just don't.

Do not take this class if you don't need. He will never give anyone more than an A- in this class because his standards are way too high. He deducts marks from assignments and projects for the unfair reasons and expects you to remember everything he says for tests and exams. It was soo much effort for a 3 credit class
Worst course I have ever taken.
I would not recommend this class.

I liked the course structure; many lecture slides corresponded to the reading headings. Paul also brought learned theories back to illustrate the weekly content, showing how various theories were applied. The final exam was fair; it covered material from the readings and what he taught in class. Overall, Paul made me feel prepared and confident.

I highly recommend avoiding Dr. Yeung's class. He is passionate about his work, but his lectures are extremely content-heavy while being unpalatable and difficult to understand. The final exam had several questions that were unrelated to the emphasized content. He also made strange personal comments towards me that I found quite unprofessional.

Prepare to learn all the course material yourself. His lectures are disorganized and he tends to ramble on for three hours. His rubric's don't align with his grading, and will dock marks for things he never mentioned. Good luck on the final exam, he's testing you on concepts he neglected to teach (and your hand will cramp up from all the writing).

im six hours away from taking this exam and its messed up. Paul as a person is very kind and loving BUT this exam is going to go so bad! his lecture slides are very un clear and there is just to much content to get through. if you are planning of taking it as a elective DONT TAKE IT. the essays weren't that bad tho.

Everything about the class is so unclear.  I felt like I learned nothing about career counselling the whole semester.  Im confused in the lectures all the time and have no idea what to do for the assignments.  There is a final exam too which im probably going to fail.  SAVE YOURSELF DONT TAKE!!!

Paul's a very nice prof but structures his class to very confusing and difficult to succeed in, though it isnt his intent. The syllabus, lecture slides, assignments have so many pages yet still not clear of what he expects. The final is a few weeks away and he isnt even sure what it'll be like so we cant even prepare adequately for it.

Paul is an incredibly tough grader and his grading criteria are really vague. The syllabus is extremely long and extremely confusing, maybe because there are SO MANY (time consuming) assignments in this course. AVOID

Paul is such a tough marker! I found his lectures to be dry and it was hard to retain the information. His weekly quizzes were horrendous, I'm an avid note-taker/textbook reader and I found myself getting 4/10 - 7/10 on each quiz no matter how much I studied. Even the way you have to name the file of your assignments was an unnecessary hassle!

Paul is a really nice person. But this course had a syllabus that was more than 30 pages, and even with all that the assignments were still unclear. It's hard to know what you're being graded on, and assignments are constantly being added or changed. It felt chaotic and disorganized. Avoid if you can.
